metadata.title: Build a Particle-in-a-Box Energy Ladder with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: Okabe-Ito: TEAL #009E73 = energy levels/wavefunctions, CRIMSON #D55E00 = ratio chips/annotations. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01–B04=PIB simulation segment; B05=brand outro. TTS: 'Medhavy' throughout narration.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, particle in a box, L equals one nanometer, electron mass. Draw the first three energy levels as horizontal lines. Label each with its energy in electron-volts. Three things in every generation prompt: the rule, the numbers, what to render. Miss any one and the scene needs a re-do.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  particle in a box, L = 1 nm, m = mₑ
  draw energy levels n=1,2,3 as rungs
  sine eigenstates at each rung
  label E_n in eV"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find this line: n-squared times E-one. That is the entire physics. Check that E-one is twelve-forty-squared times pi-squared over twice the mass times L-squared — in electron-volts. Check n equals one gives point-three-seven-six. Thirty seconds. Never skip this.

props.code:
# pib_L1nm.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
L = 1e-9          # m  well width (1 nm)
hbar = 1.0546e-34 # J·s
m_e  = 9.109e-31  # kg

E1 = (np.pi**2 * hbar**2) / (2 * m_e * L**2)
E1_eV = E1 / 1.602e-19   # ≈ 0.376 eV

def E_n(n):          # ← the physics: n²·E₁
    return n**2 * E1_eV

# n=1: 0.376 eV   n=2: 1.504 eV   n=3: 3.384 eV
# ratio E₂/E₁ = 4.000  (n²)  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the ratio E-two over E-one. The chip shows four-point-zero-zero-zero. Exact — not approximate. That is the n-squared law. Second: E-one sits above zero. Ground state energy is point-three-seven-six electron-volts. The particle cannot have zero energy — zero-point energy is built into the geometry. Both correct. Ready to iterate.

Iterate: claude — update pib L-one-nm dot py — halve L to point-five nanometers and show that E-one quadruples to one-point-five-zero-four electron-volts. One parameter, one check. Prompt, read, run, verify, change. The physics was the excuse. The loop is the skill you carry forward.

props.command:
claude "update pib_L1nm.py:
  → change L from 1 nm to 0.5 nm
  → show E₁ for both widths side-by-side
  → label: E₁ ∝ 1/L² (halve L → 4×E₁)"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
